turns.content: I am SO frustrated right now na ka 😤😤 Instagram is making me crazy. my reach dropped like 30% this week and I didn't do ANYTHING different!!

Ugh, that's really frustrating — especially when you haven't changed anything on your end. A sudden 30% drop usually means Instagram changed something on their side, not yours. When did you first notice the dip? And is it affecting all your posts equally or just certain types?

I notice on Tuesday na ka. I post a photo of my morning market run — usually that kind of content get like 2,000-3,000 reach. this one got 800!! EIGHT HUNDRED. I thought maybe the photo was bad so I post another one Wednesday, same thing. then I check my insights and EVERYTHING is down. reels, photo, story. all down 30%

Okay so it's across the board — that confirms it's not your content, it's the algorithm. Instagram has been rolling out changes in February and a lot of creators are reporting the same drop. You're definitely not alone in this.

A few things that might be going on:
1. Instagram is pushing Reels harder right now, so static photos get less reach
2. They changed how they rank content in the Explore page
3. There might be a "shadowban-like" glitch — sometimes accounts randomly get deprioritized for a few days

Have you been posting more Reels or mostly photos lately?

mostly photo na ka because I don't have time to edit video!! I wake up 4am, I cook all day, I clean up at night. when am I supposed to sit and edit a Reel?? Instagram want me to be a full time content creator but I am a full time FOOD VENDOR. my job is chicken rice not video editing 😤

I completely hear you — Instagram keeps adding features that basically demand you become a full-time content creator to stay visible. It's exhausting for people who actually, you know, have a real job to do.

But here's the thing: you don't need highly edited Reels. Some of the best-performing food Reels are raw and unedited. Think 15-30 second clips of:
- Pouring chicken broth over rice (the sizzle sound!)
- Slicing chicken in real time
- The moment you light the charcoal in the dark at 4am
- A time-lapse of your lunch rush line

These take almost no editing — just point, shoot, add a trending sound, done. Would that be manageable?

okay the pouring broth one is actually a good idea because the sound is SO satisfying. people love ASMR food thing right?? I can just set my phone on the cart and record while I work. but what annoy me is WHY should I have to change what I do because Instagram decide to mess with the algorithm?? last month my same content was doing fine!!

You're 100% right to be annoyed — it IS unfair. You're essentially building your business on someone else's platform and they can change the rules whenever they want without asking you. That's the ugly truth about social media.

But the practical reality is: you can either fight the algorithm or work with it. Fighting it means posting the same way and watching your reach keep dropping. Working with it means small adjustments — like those quick Reels — that don't take much extra time but keep your content visible.

Think of it like when ingredient prices go up at the market. You don't like it, but you adapt because standing still isn't an option.

ugh okay fine that is a fair point na ka. the ingredient price analogy is actually very relatable to me haha 😂 okay so what about the timing?? I usually post at night around 9-10pm because that's when I finally sit down. is that a bad time??

For a Bangkok audience, 9-10pm is actually not bad — a lot of Thai users scroll Instagram around that time. But if the algorithm is deprioritizing you right now, timing becomes more important. Try mixing it up:

- **Morning post (6-7am):** People checking their phones when they wake up. Your 4am market content is PERFECT for this slot — post right after you finish at the market.
- **Lunch time (11:30am-12:30pm):** People on lunch break thinking about food. This is golden for food content.
- **Evening (8-9pm):** Your current slot, still good.

Also, the first 30 minutes after posting are critical for the algorithm. If you get good engagement quickly, Instagram pushes it to more people. So post when your most active followers are online.
the lunch time one is smart but that is literally my busiest time na ka!! 11 to 1:30 is non stop for me. I cannot pick up my phone. maybe I can schedule the post?? is that possible??

Yes! Instagram has a built-in scheduling feature now. You can create your post the night before and schedule it to go live at noon the next day. Here's how:

1. Create your post as usual (photo, caption, hashtags, everything)
2. Before hitting "Share," tap "Advanced settings" at the bottom
3. Toggle on "Schedule" and pick the date and time
4. Confirm and it'll auto-post at that time

So you could prep your lunch-time post at 9pm when you're relaxing, schedule it for noon, and it goes live while you're busy selling plates. No interruption to your rush.

arai wa I didn't know Instagram can do that!! okay this change my life haha. I can prep everything at night and then it post by itself while I am working. why nobody tell me this before?? okay I feel less angry now. still annoyed at the algorithm but at least I have a plan na ka 😤➡️😊

That's the spirit — from 😤 to 😊! The scheduling feature is a game changer for people like you who can't be on their phone during peak hours. Set it up tonight and test it tomorrow.

And don't stress too much about the algorithm dip. These things usually bounce back within a couple of weeks, especially if you start mixing in a few quick Reels. Your content is genuinely good — the algorithm just needs a little nudge to remember that. You've got this! 🙏
